topic: Sanad offices are staffed entirely by Omani nationals — the only sector in Oman where 100% Omanisation is mandatory. Here is what that means for how they operate.
format: Open with a specific Sanad or PRO failure moment — a visa that expired, a client who found out at the airport, a missed MOL filing — make it so specific the reader stops scrolling
post:
A client called me at 7 AM from Muscat Airport.

His work permit had expired three days earlier. He didn't know. I didn't flag it. It was sitting in a WhatsApp thread from two weeks ago, buried under 40 other messages from 12 different companies.

He missed his flight. The company paid for a new ticket.

That's work permit tracking in Oman when your entire system is a shared Excel file and a group chat.

The operational reality inside most Sanad offices right now:

You're the only licensed, Omanisation-mandated gateway between your clients and every government authority in the country. MOL filings. ROP clearances. Civil Status transactions. Omanisation certificates. All of it.

Across 20+ companies. On WhatsApp.

Not because you're careless. Because nothing was ever built for what you actually do at this volume.

When you're tracking 40 work permits, 15 visa renewals, and 8 MOL filings simultaneously, you need alerts that fire before the expiry — not after someone's already stuck at the departure gate.

That's what SmartPRO Hub is built for. And our free Sanad AI Assistant gives you instant answers on work permits, visas, and verified government fees right now — no login, no setup.

Try the Sanad AI Assistant free — instant answers on work permits, visas & government fees: https://www.thesmartpro.io/sanad/assistant?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=sanad-pro
